response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is an accomplished full-stack and web software engineer with 13+ years of experience, but her background has virtually no overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in her resume. Her closest adjacent work is low-latency software at Sociomantic (async I/O, caching) and recent AI-agent tooling (MCP frameworks), but these are software-layer concerns far removed from the silicon-level performance analysis and deep-learning workload optimization this role requires.
strengths: 13+ years of broad software engineering experience across embedded, web, and systems domains, meeting the BS+8yr education threshold
Demonstrated performance-conscious engineering in high-throughput environments (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Sociomantic using async I/O and low-latency patterns)
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and agentic workflows at re:cinq, showing awareness of the AI ecosystem
concerns: Zero hardware experience: no SoC/IP modeling, no GPU architecture, no SIMD/MIMD, no AI accelerator work of any kind
No background in deep neural network training or inference optimization, which is a core preferred qualification for this role
Principal-level IC roles in this domain typically require PhD-caliber depth in computer architecture or hardware design; candidate's education and career trajectory are entirely software-focused
